evidence: Runtime dependency wiring is spread across large function-pointer bags and repeated merge logic (`goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_ops.go`, `goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_core.go`, `goseg/broadcast/collectors/collectors_runtime.go`, `goseg/docker/orchestration/container/netdata.go`).

System concerns are mixed in broad modules that combine state management, shell/process orchestration, filesystem mutation, and metrics collection (`goseg/system/system.go`, `goseg/system/disk.go`, `goseg/system/wifi.go`).

Multiple packages rely on mutable singleton-like globals for runtime behavior (`goseg/logger/logger.go`, `goseg/session/session.go`, `goseg/config/version.go`), increasing hidden coupling and lifecycle sensitivity.

findings.summary: Runtime composition relies on oversized function-field bags and manual field copying, creating structural drift risk.
evidence: `Runtime*Ops` in `runtime_ops.go` defines many nested structs of function pointers and default builders, then `runtime_core.go` manually reprojects selected fields into narrower runtimes.
`collectorRuntime` and `NetdataRuntime` repeat the same pattern: large callback structs plus custom merge/require helpers.
Adding/changing one dependency requires synchronized edits in multiple runtime bundles and wrappers, indicating weak abstraction leverage.
findings.suggestion: Refactor to bounded interfaces per workflow (e.g., lifecycle, image metadata, config persistence) and inject cohesive service objects instead of broad callback bags; generate or centralize wiring to remove manual field projection.

findings.summary: Default/override merge and dependency-guard logic is duplicated across modules with inconsistent shape.
evidence: `withWiFiRuntime`, `NewNetworkRuntimeWith`, and `mergeCollector*Runtime` each implement ad-hoc nil/zero override merging.
`NetdataRuntime` has multiple overlapping `require*` methods (`requireConfigWriter`, `requireCopyDependency`) that repeat the same dependency checks and error semantics.
The repeated hand-rolled merge/guard style increases maintenance overhead and inconsistency when dependencies evolve.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a single option-validation pattern (constructor + validate) reused across runtime structs, and collapse duplicate dependency checks into one canonical validator per runtime.

findings.summary: The `system` layer mixes unrelated operational domains, reducing clear ownership boundaries.
evidence: `system.go` combines host metrics, NP box provisioning behavior, and cron job mutation.
`disk.go` combines SMART health evaluation, filesystem formatting/fstab mutation, and tmp symlink relocation logic.
`wifi.go` combines command execution adapters, radio/network control, long-running state loops, and global runtime state management.
findings.suggestion: Split `system` into focused submodules/services (e.g., host_metrics, storage_provisioning, wifi_runtime, maintenance_tasks) and expose a thin orchestrator API to reduce multi-responsibility files.

findings.summary: Mutable process-wide state is used as a primary design mechanism, creating hidden coupling and order-dependent behavior.
evidence: `logger.go` keeps global mutable initialization state (`LogPath`, `loggerInitState`, `loggerInitErr`, sink globals) and mutates global zap logger in `InitializeWithRuntime`.
`session.go` stores active runtime/session state in package globals with mutable replacement APIs (`active`, `SetLogstreamRuntime`, `SetClientManager`).
`version.go` uses mutable package globals (`versionStore`, HTTP client, retry policy) altered via setters, making behavior context-dependent across callers/tests.
findings.suggestion: Move mutable runtime state into explicit service instances created in composition root; pass those services via constructors/interfaces rather than package globals.

findings.summary: Transition handling logic is fragmented across parallel maps/builders, increasing drift risk between event routing and state reduction.
evidence: `rectify.go` defines multiple reducer maps (`urbitTransitionReducers`, `newShipTransitionReducers`, `systemTransitionReducers`, `startramTransitionReducers`) plus routing maps.
`urbit_operations_runtime.go` defines a separate transition runner map and many per-transition step builders for related transition types.
Transition additions require changes in multiple registries/locations, so coherence depends on manual synchronization.
findings.suggestion: Define a single declarative transition registry (type -> command handler -> reducer metadata) used by both workflow execution and broadcast reduction, with invariant tests that verify registry completeness.
